rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROBERTA PERSON FAMILY TRUST DATED DECEMBER 23, 1999
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Claim Of Equitable Indemnity Re: Order For Surcharge
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/6/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: In addition to the issues posted by the prior examiner in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 10/24/22, which do not yet appear to have been addressed or fully addressed, the petition is missing the names and ADDRESSES of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] The register of actions shows objections filed on 10/18/22 by Rochelle Black-Hawkins. No courtesy copy of the objections has been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] (How does she have standing in these proceedings?) Additional papers have been filed since the last hearing by Petitioner Frank Lewis, courtesy copies of which have not yet been received by the examiner. Courtesy copies of all responsive papers must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/25/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROBERTA PERSON FAMILY TRUST DATED DECEMBER 23, 1999
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/6/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: In addition to the remaining issue posted by the prior examiner in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 10/24/22, the successor trustee must address the following issues: 1. For what accounting services was Jonathan Arnold paid $63.75 on 6/22/20? If the Court should grant fees based on 1% of the FMV, any payments to another party to prepare the court accounting must be deducted from the trustee fees. 2. The receipt for the distribution to Jerome Black was signed by his attorney. The 7/15/20 order for preliminary distribution did not order his equalizing distribution to be made in care of his attorney. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/25/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: RICHARD PATUREL REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/6/23. The amended account petition was filed on 10/13/22 (but not actually e-filed until 10/17/22, when it was first available for viewing). In any event, trusts require 30 days' notice. The assets on hand should be exactly the assets shown on Schedule C of the conservatorship account as having been transferred ("disbursed") to the trust on 10/14/20 and 7/30/21 as no I&A was filed in this case. An amended pleading means a pleading that completely restates and supersedes the pleading it amends for all purposes. [CRC 7.3(3).] There should be no reference to "changes" or a "previous valuation" or the "last accounting" when this is the first accounting. The reported market value in paragraph 16 of the report does not agree with the market value shown on the schedule of "Estimated Market Value." Changes in the form of assets, such as dividends reinvested, stock splits, or changes in the name of a security, should be shown on an informational schedule; not in the body of the report. It appears that a second amended accounting should be filed. Exhibit B is not an itemization of attorney costs, and the costs were incurred in the conservatorship case, not this trust. Trustee time entries on 9/8/20, 10/14/20, 11/11/20, 3/23/21, 4/13/21, 8/11/21, and 8/19/21 were also billed in the conservatorship case. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 1/25/23. If the amended accounting petition cannot be filed in time to give 30 days' notice before 2/6/23, Counsel should contact the examiner to request more time. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GIUSEPPE S. ORTENZO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/21/2022 (no earlier date available). The following issue must be addressed: The surviving spouse appears to have made an unqualified 13502 election to probate her intestate share of community property passing to her, as well as her share of community property belonging to her. If the tenancy in common (TIC) interests in the subject real property held by the decedent and the surviving spouse were community property, the inventory and appraisal must be corrected to include the TIC interest held in the surviving spouse's name, in addition to the TIC interest held in the decedent's name, unless the surviving spouse establishes that the TIC interest held in her name is her separate property not subject to the 13502 election already made. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

Counsel for the suspended trustee to report (1) why the suspended trustee's accounting has not been filed despite the court order entered on 10/10/22; (2) if any and all trust assets have been turned to the temporary successor trustee as ordered on 10/10/22; (3) if a list of assets has been provided to the temporary successor trustee; (4) status of the accounting. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TONKIN GRANDCHILDRENS TRUST OF 1999 FBO ASHTON TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To A)Establish Existence, Terms, And Validity Of Trust; B)Confirm Assets Of The Trust; And C) Appoint Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/6/23. No earlier availability. No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] No courtesy copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] A quick skim of the proof of service online shows that the wrong form of notice was served if this is, in fact, a petition under Probate Code Section 850 (to confirm assets to a trust) and that notice was mailed to someone in care of another. Notice must be mailed individually and directly to the person entitled to notice unless the person entitled to notice has directed in writing to give notice in care of another. [CRC 7.51(a).] If the person is a minor, notice must be given directly to the minor and also to the person having legal custody of the minor with whom the minor resides. [CRC 7.51(d).] The proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and all supporting papers must be received by the assigned examiner by 1/25/23 to avoid another continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARITAL TRUST CREATED UNDER THE JAMES FAMILY TRUST, DATED SEPTEMBER 13, 1973
rulings.Calendar Matter: First Account And Report Of Trustee; Petition For Approval Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/18/2023 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. New notice must be given. 2) A copy of the order that admitted the surviving trustor's will to probate must be filed 3) No Attachments A1-A4 were filed. 4) No separate schedule was filed for the trust's interest in GBJ Family Investment Partners, LP specifically gifted by the surviving trustor's power of appointment. 5) The petitioner became successor trustee more than five years ago. Has the petitioner provided informal annual accountings to the beneficiaries? If not, please explain why the petitioner's first accounting was delayed. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SUM CHUEN TO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/6/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately, conventionally, and confidentially. If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Probate Code Sec. 8113.] Step-siblings or their issue would not be heirs. Children of the decedent's father are the decedent's HALF-siblings and heirs. Ka Chung To has not waived bond. Under what authority did Ka Yun To, the son of Wai Keung To, sign the waiver of bond on behalf of his deceased father? Either certified Letters issued to him as the appointed personal representative of his father's estate or a copy of a certification of trust that shows that he was named as the successor trustee of Wai Keung To's trust, or some equivalent thereto, must be filed. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/25/23. (This Court no longer requires the local form, Declaration of Real Property.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES T. PORRAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/6/23. No earlier availability. No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] No courtesy copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] No proof of publication. The name and RELATIONSHIP to the decedent must be stated for each person listed in item 8 of the petition, which should include the person named in item 2 of the petition. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] The decedent's parents' names and dates of death must be set forth. The proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and all supporting papers must be received by the assigned examiner by 1/25/23 to avoid another continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
